subject: 2023-2024 Request for Proposals for translational and early clinical melanoma research with emphasis on Team Science Awards, Young Investigator Awards, Pilot Awards, Dermatology Career Development Awards, and Special Opportunity Awards

content.title: Award Mechanisms and Funding Levels
subtitles: Team Science Awards: $300,000 per year, $900,000 total over 3 years
Young Investigator Awards: $85,000 per year, $255,000 total over 3 years
Pilot Awards: $50,000 per year, $100,000 total over 2 years
Dermatology Career Development Awards: $85,000 per year, $170,000 total over 2 years (NEW for 2023-2024)
Academic-Industry Partnership Award available for Team Science proposals
Special Opportunity Awards in: Acral Melanoma, Brain Metastasis, Dermatology Technologies, Immunology, Radiation Oncology, Uveal Melanoma, UK/Israel-based researchers
Total new funding planned: at least $11.5 million in 2023-2024 cycle

content.title: Application Submission and Access Management
subtitles: Deadline: 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time, proposals not considered after deadline
Must use ProposalCentral online application tool at https://ProposalCentral.com/
Use document templates and requirements provided in ProposalCentral
Enable Other Users to Access this Proposal: grant 'Edit' access to institutional signing officials ahead of time
Electronic signatures required from Signing Official and PI for submission
Grant access to Signing Official well in advance to avoid last-minute submission issues

content.title: Title Page and Project Information
subtitles: Enter project title - will become public if funded, no proprietary information allowed
Select Special Opportunity Award subprogram if applicable (otherwise N/A)
For Academic-Industry Partnership proposals: include name of Industry Partner on title page
PI name and institution become public if awarded

content.title: Key Personnel and Roles
subtitles: Administrative PI (Team Science only): team leader, primary MRA contact, shares scientific leadership, ensures compliance with award terms
Principal Investigator (all applications): applicant for Pilot and Young Investigator; shares authority equally with Administrative PI for Team Science
Co-Investigator: vital scientific contributors with measurable effort, designated as Key Personnel
Collaborator: lesser role than PI/Co-I, may be designated as Key Personnel if compensated
Mentor (Team Science and Young Investigator): established investigator at same institution, provides support and career guidance, expertise in melanoma research strongly advised
Young Investigator (Team Science): within first 5 years of first independent faculty appointment at Assistant Professor level, integral to proposal aims
Consultant: provides guidance on specific aspects, may be designated as Key Personnel
Other Key Personnel: Project Managers, Technicians, Postdocs, Fellows, Research Assistants, Graduate Students if contributing substantively
ORCID IDs required for: PI, Young Investigator, Mentor, Administrative PI

content.title: Organization and Institution Information
subtitles: Key information about PI's institution required
Name and email of signing official who will be contacted if funded
ROR (Research Organization Registry ID) should be included if institution has one
Signing official must have at least 'Edit' access in ProposalCentral to sign application
Administrative PI's institution oversees all organization assurances and certifications (Team Science)

content.title: Data and Renewable Reagent Sharing Plan
subtitles: Describe types of data and renewable reagents that will be generated
Explain how data and reagents will be shared to promote rapid research advancement, transparency, reproducibility, collaboration
MRA recommends posting manuscripts to pre-print server ahead of or at journal submission
MRA recommends posting research outputs (data, code, software) to public repositories when generated
MRA recommends publishing in open-access journals
MRA recommends all research outputs use CC BY-NC license (no commercial modification rights)
MRA requires final accepted publication deposited in PubMed Central, available 12 months after publication
MRA requires data, code, software for independent verification made freely and publicly available at publication
MRA will cover policy compliance costs (article processing charges, data storage) if included in original budget

content.title: Abstracts and Keywords
subtitles: Provide general audience abstract (non-technical): 2,000 characters including spaces maximum
Provide technical abstract: 2,000 characters including spaces maximum
Include keywords for both abstracts
General audience abstract will become public if funded - no proprietary information

content.title: Budget Requirements and Justification
subtitles: Enter budget details for each award period requested
Awards will NOT support indirect costs, overhead costs, or similar institutional charges
Fringe benefits for personnel salaries ARE allowable
Include costs for MRA data sharing policy compliance
Academic-Industry Partnership: budget must contain ALL costs from both MRA and industry partner, with contributions clearly explained
Provide sufficient detail for evaluation of major budget portions (2,000 character limit in ProposalCentral forms)
If more space needed than ProposalCentral forms allow, upload budget justification document in attachments (step #13)
Do not exceed yearly and total budget caps for each award mechanism

content.title: Current and Pending Research Support
subtitles: List all current and pending support for Applicant (required for all)
Team Science: also list support for Principal Investigators and Young Investigators ONLY
Young Investigator and Pilot: current/pending support required for Applicant only
Any overlap with MRA proposal must be described and explained
Add support to Professional Profile in ProposalCentral Step #6: Other Support
For Key Personnel: if they grant 'View' access, select their support from profile; otherwise use template from Step #2 and upload in Step #13

content.title: Organizational Assurances
subtitles: IRB approval if applicable - not required at application but required before initial payments
IACUC approval if applicable

content.title: Required Attachments: Biosketches and Support Documentation
subtitles: Biosketch: NIH format for PI and all Key Personnel listed in Step #6
Biosketches for research support staff, students, postdocs, other training positions NOT required
Applicants without NIH biosketch may use template in ProposalCentral
MRA welcomes inclusion of research outputs: datasets, code, patents, papers on preprint servers (in addition to publications)
Current and pending research support template (Team Science ONLY): use only for Key Personnel where applicant lacks ProposalCentral access to their support

content.title: Project Description Requirements
subtitles: Must be formatted: Arial 11-point OR Times New Roman 12-point font
Margins: no less than 1/2 inch (0.5 inches)
Page limit: 5 pages maximum

Inclusive of: Background and specific aims, Preliminary data, Experimental design and methods, Statistical plan, Figures (embedded within sections), Rationale/fit with key criteria including potential for clinical impact

content.title: Literature References
subtitles: Up to 30 references supporting the project description
Allowed IN ADDITION to the 5-page project description limit
Separate attachment from project description

content.title: Mentor Letters and Eligibility Documentation
subtitles: Mentor Letter of Support required for: Young Investigator, Team Science, Dermatology Career Development Award proposals
Letter should confirm applicant has independent research program
Include brief statement about: the applicant, Mentor's role, mentoring plan, research environment, institutional support sources
MRA recommends considering unconscious bias guidelines when preparing support letters (https://tinyurl.com/)
Applicant Eligibility Checklist required: signature from Department Chair, Division Head, or Dean
Young Investigator checklist and Dermatology Career Development checklist accessible via provided links
Young Investigator and Dermatology Career Development: email checklist to kackerman@curemelanoma.org by October 20, 2023, also upload copy to application
Team Science: upload eligibility checklist as attachment in ProposalCentral

content.title: Academic-Industry Partnership Requirements
subtitles: Include letter of support from industry partner with minimum requirements:
Description of total support amount: clearly indicate monetary and/or in-kind portions for full award term
Must match total funding requested in application
Acknowledgement: if Award made, industry partner will execute agreement with awardee Institution within 90 days
Acknowledgement: if Award made, industry partner will execute agreement with MRA within 90 days
Example template agreement available from MRA upon request (contact kackerman@curemelanoma.org)

content.title: Clinical Trial Protocol Requirements
subtitles: For proposals involving clinical trials: attach brief protocol synopsis
Include timeline with milestones
IRB approval not required at application time
IRB approval IS required before initial payments are made

categories.name: Award Mechanisms
examples: Team Science Awards ($900K/3 years)
Young Investigator Awards ($255K/3 years)
Pilot Awards ($100K/2 years)
Dermatology Career Development Awards ($170K/2 years)
Academic-Industry Partnership
Special Opportunity Awards
Total $11.5M+ funding available

categories.name: Eligibility Requirements
examples: Young Investigator: within 5 years of first independent faculty appointment
Young Investigator: Assistant Professor level or equivalent
Team Science: must include at least one Young Investigator
Young Investigator work must be integral to proposal aims
Applicant Eligibility Checklist required with Department Chair/Division Head/Dean signature
Mentor required: established investigator at same institution
Mentor expertise in melanoma research strongly advised
IRB approval required before initial payment

categories.name: Application Submission
examples: Deadline: 11:59 PM Eastern Time
ProposalCentral online tool required
Electronic signatures required (PI + Signing Official)
Grant 'Edit' access to Signing Official in advance
Use provided document templates
No proposals accepted after deadline

categories.name: Key Personnel Roles
examples: Administrative PI (Team Science): team leader and primary contact
Principal Investigator required for all applications
Co-Investigator: vital scientific contributor with measurable effort
Mentor: provides support and career guidance
Young Investigator must be designated as Key Personnel
Consultant may be designated as Key Personnel
ORCID IDs required: PI, Young Investigator, Mentor, Administrative PI
Biosketches required for all Key Personnel
Current/Pending Support required for PI and Young Investigators

categories.name: Data Sharing Policy
examples: Post manuscripts to pre-print server (recommended)
Post research outputs to public repositories when generated (recommended)
Publish in open-access journals (recommended)
Use CC BY-NC license for all research outputs (recommended)
Deposit final publication in PubMed Central within 12 months (required)
Make verification data/code/software publicly available at publication (required)
MRA covers compliance costs if budgeted
Promotes rapid advancement, transparency, reproducibility, collaboration

categories.name: Budget Constraints
examples: NO indirect costs, overhead, or institutional charges allowed
Fringe benefits for personnel salaries ARE allowable
Include data sharing policy compliance costs
Academic-Industry Partnership: clearly explain contributions from each party
Do not exceed yearly/total budget caps for mechanism

constraints.value: Arial 11-point OR Times New Roman 12-point font
No indirect costs or overhead allowed
